claim: Multimodal RAG systems can retrieve across multiple data types (images, audio, video) by embedding all modalities into a shared vector space, enabling cross-modal search.
actionable: Extract text from images via OCR, audio via ASR, and video via vision-language models, then embed all modalities into the same vector space for unified retrieval.
source_title: Turn your multimodal data into something you can actually query

claim: Vision-language models can generate timestamped descriptions from video segments, providing additional visual context beyond what audio and images alone capture.
actionable: Use a vision-language model to process video frames and generate timestamped descriptions that capture visual details, then include these in your multimodal RAG system.
source_title: Turn your multimodal data into something you can actually query

claim: ASR and OCR are the foundational techniques for converting audio and images into LLM-ready text for pipelines.
actionable: Apply automatic speech recognition to audio files and optical character recognition to images to produce text that feeds into your multimodal data pipeline.
source_title: Turn your multimodal data into something you can actually query
